subject: Add more coverage to drools-pdp health check
commit_message:
Add more coverage to drools-pdp health check

Added getProperties() method to Factory, as the system properties could
not be found in the jenkins build, for some reason.
Removed Factory, as not needed for junit testing.

Change-Id: I5070b9502ae06be6995cccbb005653928591512c
Issue-ID: POLICY-1148
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/640/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/640/ )

There are test failures. Please look in the log to identify them. ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/640/ )

Question Jim, is it possible that instead of creating this Factory proxy object to satisfy junits, to instantiate the HealthcheckMonitor implementation classes directly for the coverage?

Patch Set 3: Verified+1

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

> Question Jim, is it possible that instead of creating this Factory
 > proxy object to satisfy junits, to instantiate the
 > HealthcheckMonitor implementation classes directly for the
 > coverage?

Good point.  Since it isn't static and the methods aren't used by the constructor, I've removed the Factory class and moved its methods into the Monitor class.
Change has been successfully merged by Pamela Dragosh
